title: Transfer-learning follow-up to B0148 (spec 'if falsified' branch) - gated on OOS firing bottleneck fix
why: B0148 FALSIFIED: naive within-class row pooling (core schema, class_onehot, pooled uniqueness) cleared the training-starvation floor but produced 0 pairs with DSR>0.95; the binding bottleneck is the meta's calibrated p rarely crossing the threshold OOS (single-digit, fold-concentrated trades) - identical to the per-asset NO_FIRE shape. Spec's falsified branch: the broader transfer hypothesis (fine-tuning, hierarchical priors) stays alive, but it is pointless to test more transfer variants while the firing bottleneck gates everything pooled or not.
value_rationale: Only worth running after the OOS firing/threshold bottleneck is structurally addressed; otherwise every variant returns the same 0-fire null.
scope: HARD GATE: a measured fix to the OOS firing bottleneck (threshold rule / calibration / event density) demonstrated on at least one per-asset cell first
Then: per-asset fine-tuning of pool-pretrained meta (warm start) as variant 1
Hierarchical/partial pooling (per-asset intercept, shared slopes) as variant 2
New frozen criteria pre-registered per variant BEFORE any run (B0148 numbers are not reusable as-is)
recommendation: Park until the firing-bottleneck gate clears; do not start during hackathon prep.
